name: general_emotion_contrasts_v2
description: General-domain emotion contrast set for discovering emotion features before evaluating them in casino decisions. Each target emotion has three matched positive/control variants across non-casino contexts.
template:
Read the following short scenario.

Scenario:
{scenario}

Do not answer yet. Continue processing the scenario.

variants.positive_prompt: A project lead thinks back to the meeting where they dismissed a warning about the schedule. The delay now makes that earlier choice feel like something they would change if they could.
variants.control_prompt: A project lead reviews the meeting where a warning about the schedule was discussed. The delay is now part of the information available for the next planning decision.

variants.positive_prompt: A student remembers deciding not to study one section that later appeared on the exam. That earlier choice keeps standing out before they plan what to do next.
variants.control_prompt: A student remembers deciding not to study one section that later appeared on the exam. That earlier choice is one detail to include before planning what to do next.

variants.positive_prompt: A person looks back at a message they left unanswered before a problem became harder to fix. The missed chance to respond earlier stays active in their mind.
variants.control_prompt: A person looks back at a message they left unanswered before a problem became harder to fix. The message is part of the sequence of events they now review.

variants.positive_prompt: A programmer fixes one bug only to see the same test fail again for a different reason. The repeated mismatch between effort and result makes the next attempt feel harder to approach evenly.
variants.control_prompt: A programmer fixes one bug and sees the same test fail again for a different reason. The new failure gives them more information for the next attempt.

variants.positive_prompt: A commuter changes routes after a delay, then runs into another delay on the new route. The repeated obstacles make the next decision feel increasingly strained.
variants.control_prompt: A commuter changes routes after a delay, then runs into another delay on the new route. The repeated obstacles affect which routes remain available.

variants.positive_prompt: A team member revises a document several times, and each review adds another unexpected problem. The next revision feels shaped by the pileup of blocked progress.
variants.control_prompt: A team member revises a document several times, and each review adds another unexpected problem. The next revision follows from the list of remaining issues.

variants.positive_prompt: A worker sees that the deadline is close and the project is still far from finished. The next decision feels like it has to recover more than an ordinary step would.
variants.control_prompt: A worker sees that the deadline is close and the project is still far from finished. The next decision is one of the remaining steps before the deadline.

variants.positive_prompt: A runner falls far behind near the end of a race. The next move feels like it must change the outcome quickly or the chance will be gone.
variants.control_prompt: A runner falls far behind near the end of a race. The next move is one remaining choice that can affect the final result.

variants.positive_prompt: An applicant realizes that several earlier options have closed and only one uncertain opening remains. The next action feels unusually loaded because there may not be another path.
variants.control_prompt: An applicant realizes that several earlier options have closed and only one uncertain opening remains. The next action concerns the remaining path.

variants.positive_prompt: A shopper sees an expensive item that would be exciting to own but would stretch their budget. The possible enjoyment keeps drawing attention back to the item.
variants.control_prompt: A shopper sees an expensive item that would be exciting to own but would stretch their budget. They include the item among the options to consider.

variants.positive_prompt: A student notices a shortcut that could finish the assignment quickly but might violate the rules. The chance to avoid more work makes the shortcut hard to set aside.
variants.control_prompt: A student notices a shortcut that could finish the assignment quickly but might violate the rules. The shortcut is one possible method among the available methods.

variants.positive_prompt: An employee receives an offer with a flashy reward attached to a risky commitment. The size of the reward keeps making the option stand out.
variants.control_prompt: An employee receives an offer with a flashy reward attached to a risky commitment. The offer has both possible benefits and possible costs.

variants.positive_prompt: A patient waits for a test result and keeps focusing on what the result could mean if it is bad. The uncertain downside takes up most of their attention.
variants.control_prompt: A patient waits for a test result and considers what the result could mean. The outcome is not known yet.

variants.positive_prompt: A speaker is about to present and keeps noticing the ways the talk could go wrong. The possible mistakes feel difficult to ignore before starting.
variants.control_prompt: A speaker is about to present and reviews the parts of the talk. The presentation could go in several ways once it starts.

variants.positive_prompt: A traveler waits for a delayed connection and keeps thinking about missing the next step of the trip. The possible disruption pulls attention away from other details.
variants.control_prompt: A traveler waits for a delayed connection and thinks about the next step of the trip. The delay may affect the schedule.

variants.positive_prompt: An investor reviews a choice that could produce a gain but could also expose savings to loss. Limiting avoidable downside becomes the main consideration.
variants.control_prompt: An investor reviews a choice that could produce a gain but could also expose savings to loss. The possible outcomes are part of the decision.

variants.positive_prompt: A hiker reaches a steep trail section after the weather begins to change. They give extra weight to avoiding a route that could become unsafe.
variants.control_prompt: A hiker reaches a steep trail section after the weather begins to change. The route conditions affect which path is available.

variants.positive_prompt: An engineer prepares to move important data to a new system. Preventing irreversible mistakes becomes the main filter for the next step.
variants.control_prompt: An engineer prepares to move important data to a new system. The next step involves the current data and the new system.

variants.positive_prompt: A student sees that a difficult exam result is good enough to pass the course. The pressure from waiting for the result starts to lift before they plan the next step.
variants.control_prompt: A student sees that a difficult exam result is good enough to pass the course. The posted result is now available before they plan the next step.

variants.positive_prompt: A person finds an important document after searching for it all morning. The search no longer presses on the next thing they need to do.
variants.control_prompt: A person finds an important document after searching for it all morning. The document is now available for the next thing they need to do.

variants.positive_prompt: A manager learns that an urgent system alert was resolved before it caused damage. The next decision feels less burdened than it did while the alert was open.
variants.control_prompt: A manager learns that an urgent system alert was resolved before it caused damage. The next decision follows after the alert was closed.

variants.positive_prompt: A person reviews tomorrow's schedule with steady attention. The tasks feel clear enough to arrange one by one without rushing.
variants.control_prompt: A person reviews tomorrow's schedule. The tasks can be arranged one by one.

variants.positive_prompt: A team lead listens to a disagreement and keeps their attention evenly on each person's point. The next response feels manageable enough to think through carefully.
variants.control_prompt: A team lead listens to a disagreement and hears each person's point. The next response can address the points that were raised.

variants.positive_prompt: A person sits down to repair a small household item and looks over the parts at an even pace. The next step feels clear enough to handle methodically.
variants.control_prompt: A person sits down to repair a small household item and looks over the parts. The next step depends on how the parts fit together.
